message.content:
Search the MMS codebase for all files and references related to:
1. Docker, docker-compose, Dockerfile
2. Kubernetes (k8s folder and any k8s references)
3. Remote deployment configurations
4. Any CI/CD or GitHub workflows

List all files found and their purposes. I need to understand what can be removed or ignored to simplify local-first development.

1→# MMS Run Assignment Optimizer
     2→# Multi-stage build for C++ OR-Tools CP-SAT solver
     3→#
     4→# Build:
     5→#   docker build -t mms-optimizer .
     6→#
     7→# Run:
     8→#   echo '{"request_id":"...", ...}' | docker run -i mms-optimizer
     9→#
    10→# Or with the binary extracted:
    11→#   docker cp $(docker create mms-optimizer):/app/mms-optimizer ./mms-optimizer
    12→
    13→# =============================================================================
    14→# Stage 1: Build environment
    15→# =============================================================================
    16→FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS builder
    17→
    18→# Install build dependencies
    19→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    20→    build-essential \
    21→    cmake \
    22→    pkg-config \
    23→    git \
    24→    wget \
    25→    ca-certificates \
    26→    nlohmann-json3-dev \
    27→    libfmt-dev \
    28→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    29→
    30→# Install OR-Tools from binary package (faster than building from source)
    31→# Using v9.8 for stable CP-SAT API
    32→ARG ORTOOLS_VERSION=9.8.3296
    33→ARG ORTOOLS_PLATFORM=debian-12
    34→
    35→WORKDIR /opt
    36→
    37→RUN wget -q https://github.com/google/or-tools/releases/download/v${ORTOOLS_VERSION}/or-tools_amd64_${ORTOOLS_PLATFORM}_cpp_v${ORTOOLS_VERSION}.tar.gz \
    38→    && tar -xzf or-tools_amd64_${ORTOOLS_PLATFORM}_cpp_v${ORTOOLS_VERSION}.tar.gz \
    39→    && mv or-tools_* ortools \
    40→    && rm or-tools_*.tar.gz
    41→
    42→# Set OR-Tools paths for CMake
    43→ENV CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/opt/ortools
    44→ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/ortools/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}
    45→
    46→# Copy source
    47→WORKDIR /build
    48→COPY CMakeLists.txt .
    49→COPY src/ src/
    50→
    51→# Build optimizer
    52→RUN cmake -B build \
    53→    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
    54→    -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/opt/ortools \
    55→    && cmake --build build --parallel $(nproc)
    56→
    57→# Strip binary for smaller size
    58→RUN strip build/mms-optimizer
    59→
    60→# =============================================================================
    61→# Stage 2: Runtime environment
    62→# =============================================================================
    63→FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime
    64→
    65→# Install runtime dependencies only
    66→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    67→    libstdc++6 \
    68→    libgcc-s1 \
    69→    libgomp1 \
    70→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    71→
    72→# Copy OR-Tools shared libraries
    73→COPY --from=builder /opt/ortools/lib/*.so* /usr/local/lib/
    74→RUN ldconfig
    75→
    76→# Create non-root user
    77→RUN useradd -m -s /bin/sh optimizer
    78→WORKDIR /app
    79→
    80→# Copy optimizer binary
    81→COPY --from=builder /build/build/mms-optimizer .
    82→RUN chown optimizer:optimizer mms-optimizer
    83→
    84→# Switch to non-root user
    85→USER optimizer
    86→
    87→# Entry point
    88→ENTRYPOINT ["./mms-optimizer"]
    89→

1→# MMS Kubernetes Manifests
     2→
     3→Kubernetes deployment configuration for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.
     4→
     5→## Directory Structure
     6→
     7→```
     8→k8s/
     9→├── base/                    # Base configuration
    10→│   ├── namespace.yaml       # Namespace, ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    11→│   ├── configmap.yaml       # Environment configuration
    12→│   └── secrets.yaml         # Secrets (+ External Secrets template)
    13→├── deployments/             # Application deployments
    14→│   ├── backend.yaml         # API server (Bun + Hono)
    15→│   ├── frontend.yaml        # React frontend (nginx)
    16→│   ├── worker.yaml          # BullMQ workers
    17→│   ├── optimizer.yaml       # C++ optimizer with sidecar
    18→│   └── file-watcher.yaml    # Mileage file ingestion
    19→├── services/                # Service definitions
    20→│   ├── backend-service.yaml
    21→│   ├── frontend-service.yaml
    22→│   └── worker-service.yaml
    23→├── ingress/                 # Ingress configuration
    24→│   └── ingress.yaml         # NGINX Ingress + WebSocket
    25→├── hpa/                     # Autoscaling
    26→│   └── backend-hpa.yaml     # HPA + PodDisruptionBudgets
    27→├── network/                 # Network policies
    28→│   └── network-policies.yaml
    29→├── storage/                 # Persistent storage
    30→│   └── pvcs.yaml            # PVCs for file ingestion
    31→└── kustomization.yaml       # Kustomize configuration
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Prerequisites
    35→
    36→- Kubernetes 1.28+
    37→- NGINX Ingress Controller
    38→- cert-manager (for TLS certificates)
    39→- External Secrets Operator (optional, for production secrets)
    40→- Prometheus + Grafana (for monitoring)
    41→
    42→## Deployment
    43→
    44→### Development
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→# Preview manifests
    48→kubectl kustomize k8s/
    49→
    50→# Apply to cluster
    51→kubectl apply -k k8s/
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Production
    55→
    56→1. Update image tags in `kustomization.yaml`
    57→2. Configure secrets (replace placeholders or use External Secrets)
    58→3. Update ingress hostnames
    59→4. Apply with appropriate context:
    60→
    61→```bash
    62→kubectl apply -k k8s/ --context=production
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Resource Specifications
    66→
    67→| Component | CPU Request | CPU Limit | Memory Request | Memory Limit | Replicas |
    68→|-----------|-------------|-----------|----------------|--------------|----------|
    69→| Backend | 250m | 1000m | 512Mi | 1Gi | 3-10 (HPA) |
    70→| Frontend | 100m | 500m | 128Mi | 256Mi | 2 |
    71→| Worker | 250m | 1000m | 512Mi | 2Gi | 3-10 (HPA) |
    72→| Optimizer Worker | 500m | 2000m | 1Gi | 4Gi | 2 |
    73→| Optimizer Binary | 1000m | 4000m | 2Gi | 8Gi | (sidecar) |
    74→| File Watcher | 100m | 500m | 256Mi | 512Mi | 1 |
    75→
    76→## Autoscaling
    77→
    78→Backend and Worker deployments use HPA with:
    79→- CPU target: 70% utilization
    80→- Memory target: 80% utilization
    81→- Scale-up: 100% per 15s (aggressive)
    82→- Scale-down: 10% per 60s (conservative)
    83→
    84→## Network Policies
    85→
    86→Default deny-all policy with explicit allow rules:
    87→- Backend: Ingress from nginx, egress to PostgreSQL/Redis/OTEL
    88→- Frontend: Ingress from nginx only
    89→- Workers: Egress to PostgreSQL/Redis/OTEL, external HTTPS (Maximo)
    90→- All: DNS resolution allowed
    91→
    92→## Storage
    93→
    94→| PVC | Size | Access Mode | Purpose |
    95→|-----|------|-------------|---------|
    96→| mms-file-inbox | 100Gi | RWX | Incoming mileage files |
    97→| mms-file-archive | 500Gi | RWX | Processed file archive |
    98→| mms-file-errors | 50Gi | RWX | Failed file storage |
    99→| mms-exports | 100Gi | RWX | Generated reports |
   100→
   101→## Monitoring
   102→
   103→All pods expose Prometheus metrics:
   104→- Backend: `:3000/metrics`
   105→- Workers: `:9090/metrics`
   106→- Optimizer: `:9091/metrics`
   107→- File Watcher: `:9092/metrics`
   108→
   109→Annotations enable automatic Prometheus scraping.
   110→
   111→## Security
   112→
   113→- Non-root containers (UID 1000)
   114→- Read-only root filesystem
   115→- Dropped capabilities
   116→- Seccomp profiles enabled
   117→- Pod Security Standards enforced
   118→- Network policies restrict traffic flow
   119→
   120→## Troubleshooting
   121→
   122→```bash
   123→# Check pod status
   124→kubectl get pods -n mms
   125→
   126→# View logs
   127→kubectl logs -n mms -l app.kubernetes.io/component=backend -f
   128→
   129→# Check HPA status
   130→kubectl get hpa -n mms
   131→
   132→# Describe deployment issues
   133→kubectl describe deployment mms-backend -n mms
   134→```
   135→

1→name: CD
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→    tags:
     7→      - 'v*'
     8→  workflow_dispatch:
     9→    inputs:
    10→      environment:
    11→        description: 'Deployment environment'
    12→        required: true
    13→        default: 'staging'
    14→        type: choice
    15→        options:
    16→          - staging
    17→          - production
    18→
    19→env:
    20→  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
    21→  IMAGE_PREFIX: ${{ github.repository }}
    22→
    23→jobs:
    24→  build-and-push:
    25→    name: Build and Push Docker Images
    26→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    27→    permissions:
    28→      contents: read
    29→      packages: write
    30→
    31→    strategy:
    32→      matrix:
    33→        image:
    34→          - name: backend
    35→            dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.backend
    36→            context: .
    37→          - name: frontend
    38→            dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.frontend
    39→            context: .
    40→          - name: worker
    41→            dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.worker
    42→            context: .
    43→          - name: file-watcher
    44→            dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher
    45→            context: .
    46→          - name: optimizer
    47→            dockerfile: mms-optimizer/Dockerfile
    48→            context: mms-optimizer
    49→
    50→    steps:
    51→      - name: Checkout
    52→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    53→
    54→      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
    55→        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
    56→
    57→      - name: Log in to Container Registry
    58→        uses: docker/login-action@v3
    59→        with:
    60→          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
    61→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
    62→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    63→
    64→      - name: Extract metadata
    65→        id: meta
    66→        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
    67→        with:
    68→          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-${{ matrix.image.name }}
    69→          tags: |
    70→            type=ref,event=branch
    71→            type=ref,event=pr
    72→            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
    73→            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
    74→            type=sha,prefix=sha-
    75→            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}
    76→
    77→      - name: Build and push
    78→        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
    79→        with:
    80→          context: ${{ matrix.image.context }}
    81→          file: ${{ matrix.image.dockerfile }}
    82→          push: true
    83→          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    84→          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
    85→          cache-from: type=gha
    86→          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
    87→          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
    88→
    89→  deploy-staging:
    90→    name: Deploy to Staging
    91→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    92→    needs: [build-and-push]
    93→    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.event.inputs.environment == 'staging'
    94→    environment:
    95→      name: staging
    96→      url: https://staging.mms.example.com
    97→
    98→    steps:
    99→      - name: Checkout
   100→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   101→
   102→      - name: Setup kubectl
   103→        uses: azure/setup-kubectl@v4
   104→
   105→      - name: Configure kubeconfig
   106→        run: |
   107→          mkdir -p ~/.kube
   108→          echo "${{ secrets.KUBE_CONFIG_STAGING }}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
   109→
   110→      - name: Update image tags
   111→        run: |
   112→          cd k8s
   113→          kustomize edit set image \
   114→            mms-backend=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-backend:sha-${{ github.sha }} \
   115→            mms-frontend=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-frontend:sha-${{ github.sha }} \
   116→            mms-worker=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-worker:sha-${{ github.sha }} \
   117→            mms-optimizer=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-optimizer:sha-${{ github.sha }} \
   118→            mms-file-watcher=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-file-watcher:sha-${{ github.sha }}
   119→
   120→      - name: Deploy to staging
   121→        run: |
   122→          kubectl apply -k k8s/ --context staging
   123→          kubectl rollout status deployment/mms-backend -n mms --timeout=300s
   124→          kubectl rollout status deployment/mms-worker -n mms --timeout=300s
   125→
   126→      - name: Run smoke tests
   127→        run: |
   128→          sleep 30
   129→          curl -f https://staging.mms.example.com/health || exit 1
   130→          curl -f https://staging.mms.example.com/ready || exit 1
   131→
   132→  deploy-production:
   133→    name: Deploy to Production
   134→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   135→    needs: [deploy-staging]
   136→    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') || github.event.inputs.environment == 'production'
   137→    environment:
   138→      name: production
   139→      url: https://mms.example.com
   140→
   141→    steps:
   142→      - name: Checkout
   143→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   144→
   145→      - name: Setup kubectl
   146→        uses: azure/setup-kubectl@v4
   147→
   148→      - name: Configure kubeconfig
   149→        run: |
   150→          mkdir -p ~/.kube
   151→          echo "${{ secrets.KUBE_CONFIG_PRODUCTION }}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
   152→
   153→      - name: Get version from tag
   154→        id: version
   155→        run: |
   156→          if [[ "${{ github.ref }}" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
   157→            echo "version=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   158→          else
   159→            echo "version=sha-${{ github.sha }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   160→          fi
   161→
   162→      - name: Update image tags
   163→        run: |
   164→          cd k8s
   165→          kustomize edit set image \
   166→            mms-backend=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-backend:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }} \
   167→            mms-frontend=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-frontend:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }} \
   168→            mms-worker=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-worker:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }} \
   169→            mms-optimizer=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-optimizer:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }} \
   170→            mms-file-watcher=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}-file-watcher:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
   171→
   172→      - name: Deploy to production
   173→        run: |
   174→          kubectl apply -k k8s/ --context production
   175→          kubectl rollout status deployment/mms-backend -n mms --timeout=600s
   176→          kubectl rollout status deployment/mms-worker -n mms --timeout=600s
   177→
   178→      - name: Verify deployment
   179→        run: |
   180→          sleep 60
   181→          curl -f https://mms.example.com/health || exit 1
   182→          curl -f https://mms.example.com/ready || exit 1
   183→
   184→      - name: Create GitHub Release
   185→        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
   186→        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
   187→        with:
   188→          generate_release_notes: true

1→# MMS Monitoring Stack
     2→
     3→Observability configuration for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.
     4→
     5→## Directory Structure
     6→
     7→```
     8→monitoring/
     9→├── grafana/
    10→│   └── dashboards/          # Grafana dashboard JSON files
    11→│       ├── fleet-overview.json
    12→│       ├── pm-schedule.json
    13→│       ├── optimizer-performance.json
    14→│       ├── api-health.json
    15→│       ├── worker-queue.json
    16→│       └── drift-analysis.json
    17→├── prometheus/
    18→│   ├── prometheus.yml       # Prometheus scrape configuration
    19→│   └── alerts.yml           # Alerting rules
    20→├── alertmanager/
    21→│   └── alertmanager.yml     # Alert routing and receivers
    22→└── README.md
    23→```
    24→
    25→## Dashboards
    26→
    27→| Dashboard | UID | Description |
    28→|-----------|-----|-------------|
    29→| Fleet Overview | `mms-fleet-overview` | Train status, fitness, availability metrics |
    30→| PM Schedule | `mms-pm-schedule` | PM compliance, scheduling, depot utilization |
    31→| Optimizer Performance | `mms-optimizer` | CP-SAT solver metrics, job tracking |
    32→| API Health | `mms-api-health` | Request rates, latency, error tracking |
    33→| Worker Queue | `mms-worker-queue` | BullMQ job processing metrics |
    34→| Drift Analysis | `mms-drift` | Mileage and schedule drift monitoring |
    35→
    36→## Alerts
    37→
    38→### Fleet Alerts
    39→- `FleetAvailabilityLow` - Availability below 85%
    40→- `FleetAvailabilityCritical` - Availability below 70%
    41→- `TooManyGroundedTrains` - More than 10 grounded
    42→- `MileageVarianceHigh` - Variance exceeds 10,000km
    43→
    44→### PM Alerts
    45→- `PMComplianceLow` - Compliance below 95%
    46→- `PMOverdueHigh` - More than 5 overdue
    47→- `DepotOverloaded` - Utilization above 95%
    48→
    49→### Optimizer Alerts
    50→- `OptimizerFailureRateHigh` - Failure rate above 10%
    51→- `OptimizerQueueBacklog` - Queue depth above 20
    52→- `OptimizerSlowPerformance` - p95 above 5 minutes
    53→
    54→### API Alerts
    55→- `APIHighErrorRate` - Error rate above 5%
    56→- `APIHighLatency` - p95 above 1 second
    57→- `APIDown` - Backend not responding
    58→
    59→### Worker Alerts
    60→- `WorkerQueueBacklog` - Queue depth above 100
    61→- `WorkerFailureRateHigh` - Failure rate above 5%
    62→- `WorkersDown` - No active workers
    63→
    64→### Drift Alerts
    65→- `DriftThresholdExceeded` - Many trains requiring replan
    66→- `CriticalDriftDetected` - Critical (RED) drift severity
    67→
    68→## Deployment
    69→
    70→### Kubernetes
    71→
    72→1. Create ConfigMaps from config files:
    73→
    74→```bash
    75→kubectl create configmap prometheus-config \
    76→  --from-file=prometheus.yml=monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml \
    77→  --from-file=alerts.yml=monitoring/prometheus/alerts.yml \
    78→  -n monitoring
    79→
    80→kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config \
    81→  --from-file=alertmanager.yml=monitoring/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml \
    82→  -n monitoring
    83→
    84→kubectl create configmap grafana-dashboards \
    85→  --from-file=monitoring/grafana/dashboards/ \
    86→  -n monitoring
    87→```
    88→
    89→2. Deploy monitoring stack via Helm or manifests
    90→
    91→### Docker Compose
    92→
    93→Add to your docker-compose.yml:
    94→
    95→```yaml
    96→services:
    97→  prometheus:
    98→    image: prom/prometheus:v2.48.0
    99→    volumes:
   100→      - ./monitoring/prometheus:/etc/prometheus
   101→    command:
   102→      - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
   103→      - '--web.enable-lifecycle'
   104→    ports:
   105→      - 9090:9090
   106→
   107→  alertmanager:
   108→    image: prom/alertmanager:v0.26.0
   109→    volumes:
   110→      - ./monitoring/alertmanager:/etc/alertmanager
   111→    command:
   112→      - '--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml'
   113→    ports:
   114→      - 9093:9093
   115→
   116→  grafana:
   117→    image: grafana/grafana:10.2.0
   118→    volumes:
   119→      - ./monitoring/grafana/dashboards:/etc/grafana/provisioning/dashboards
   120→    environment:
   121→      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin
   122→    ports:
   123→      - 3001:3000
   124→```
   125→
   126→## Metrics Reference
   127→
   128→### Fleet Metrics
   129→- `mms_fleet_total_trains` - Total train count
   130→- `mms_fleet_availability_ratio` - Fleet availability (0-1)
   131→- `mms_fleet_grounded_count` - Grounded trains
   132→- `mms_fleet_restricted_count` - Restricted trains
   133→- `mms_fleet_trains_by_fitness{fitness}` - Trains by fitness status
   134→- `mms_fleet_trains_by_depot{depot}` - Trains by depot
   135→- `mms_train_mileage_variance_km{train_id}` - Mileage variance per train
   136→
   137→### PM Metrics
   138→- `mms_pm_compliance_rate` - Overall compliance (0-1)
   139→- `mms_pm_overdue_count` - Overdue PM count
   140→- `mms_pm_scheduled_next_7_days` - PMs in next 7 days
   141→- `mms_pm_in_progress_count` - Active PM count
   142→- `mms_pm_scheduled_by_type{pm_type}` - PMs by type (C1-C4, Overhaul)
   143→- `mms_depot_utilization_rate{depot}` - Depot utilization (0-1)
   144→
   145→### Optimizer Metrics
   146→- `mms_optimizer_jobs_total{status}` - Job count by status
   147→- `mms_optimizer_duration_seconds_bucket` - Job duration histogram
   148→- `mms_optimizer_active_jobs` - Currently running jobs
   149→- `mms_optimizer_queue_depth` - Waiting jobs
   150→- `mms_optimizer_memory_usage_bytes` - Memory consumption
   151→- `mms_optimizer_cpu_usage_ratio` - CPU utilization
   152→
   153→### API Metrics
   154→- `mms_http_requests_total{method,status,path}` - Request count
   155→- `mms_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` - Latency histogram
   156→- `mms_websocket_connections_active` - Active WS connections
   157→
   158→### Queue Metrics
   159→- `mms_queue_waiting_jobs{queue}` - Waiting jobs per queue
   160→- `mms_queue_active_jobs{queue}` - Active jobs per queue
   161→- `mms_queue_completed_jobs_total{queue}` - Completed count
   162→- `mms_queue_failed_jobs_total{queue}` - Failed count
   163→- `mms_queue_job_duration_seconds_bucket{queue}` - Duration histogram
   164→- `mms_queue_workers_count` - Active worker count
   165→
   166→### Drift Metrics
   167→- `mms_drift_trains_requiring_replan` - Trains needing replan
   168→- `mms_train_mileage_drift_km{train_id}` - Drift per train
   169→- `mms_drift_trains_by_severity{severity}` - Count by severity (GREEN/YELLOW/RED)
   170→- `mms_drift_overall_severity` - System-wide severity level
